changes.result: CONFIRMED — THREE DISTINCT SIGNALS LOADED, NOT TWO
changes.citation: SignalRegistry loads all packages/*/signal_library.yaml (backend/core/analytics/signal_evaluator.py lines 33-34). Three CRP-primary signals loaded at runtime: (1) signal_crp_high — knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_s24_crp_high_inflammation/signal_library.yaml line 8; activation_logic=lab_range_exceeded line 41; placeholder threshold 9999.0 line 35; activation_key=signal_crp_high::inv_crp_high_inflammation (manifest source_document=inv_crp_high_inflammation_v1.yaml, infer_source_spec_id strips _v1 per backend/core/knowledge/signal_activation_identity_v1.py lines 39-45). (2) signal_systemic_inflammation — knowledge_bus/packages/KBP-0001/signal_library.yaml line 190; activation_logic=deterministic_threshold line 227; CRP thresholds <3.0/3.0-9.99/>=10.0 mg/L lines 208-226; activation_key=signal_systemic_inflammation::KBP-0001. (3) signal_inflammation_crp_context — knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_inflammation_crp_context/signal_library.yaml line 10; activation_logic=lab_range_exceeded; placeholder threshold; activation_key=signal_inflammation_crp_context::inv_crp_context. No activation_key collision — all three keys are distinct (signal_evaluator.py lines 59-62 raises on collision; tests pass, system runs).

changes.result: CONFIRMED — NO SEMANTICALLY EQUIVALENT COMPILED PASS 3 CRP PACKAGE EXISTS
changes.citation: pkg_chronic_inflammation research_brief.yaml line 1: research_domain=inflammatory; notes lines 118-122: 'THRESHOLD DELTA WITH KBP-0001: KBP-0001 signal_systemic_inflammation uses CRP <3.0/3.0-9.99/>=10.0. Study 4 evidence supports <1.0/1.0-3.0/>=3.0 mg/L' — materially different thresholds requiring clinical sign-off. Batch_3-7_Pass_3.json files exist (5 files, Glob confirmed); Batch_4_Pass_3.json inspected via Python parse — does not contain a CRP-primary investigation spec (albumin-related spec found; CRP appears only as a supporting marker). No compiled signal_library.yaml for any Pass 3 CRP-primary package exists in the estate.

Signal authority for CRP is split across three packages with distinct activation_key values — this IS the naming split this sprint must classify. No two packages claim identical activation_key for CRP signals. knowledge_bus/packages/KBP-0001/signal_library.yaml and knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_s24_crp_high_inflammation/signal_library.yaml define different signal_ids with different activation mechanisms — these are competing authorities over CRP, which is the CF-ARCHLEG1-002 problem to be resolved.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — OUTCOME A (MIGRATION) IS VERY LIKELY UNSAFE; OUTCOME C/D/E EXPECTED
changes.citation: signal_crp_high (pkg_s24_crp_high_inflammation/signal_library.yaml line 41): activation_logic=lab_range_exceeded — fires when CRP exceeds runtime lab reference range regardless of mg/L value. signal_systemic_inflammation (KBP-0001/signal_library.yaml line 227): activation_logic=deterministic_threshold — fires based on hardcoded 3.0/10.0 mg/L. These are clinically distinct activation profiles. Migrating signal_crp_high to use signal_systemic_inflammation's logic would change WHEN the signal fires (STOP condition #3: 'Migration would change signal activation behaviour beyond package authority alignment'). Additionally, pkg_chronic_inflammation has 1.0/3.0 mg/L thresholds — migrating to it would require threshold changes (STOP condition #2). Expected safe outcome: Cursor follows Outcome C (retain legacy, classify explicitly, guard) or D/E (document distinction, test it). Outcome A is unsafe without out-of-scope threshold and behavioural changes.

signal_systemic_inflammation is the LIVE runtime CRP signal in persisted test fixtures: backend/tests/fixtures/persisted_results/lc_s20_ab_launch_core_v1.json lines 5419, 6006, 6303 — signal_id=signal_systemic_inflammation, primary_metric=crp, signal_state=suboptimal. Root cause compiler targets signal_systemic_inflammation ONLY: backend/core/knowledge/root_cause_registry_v1.py line 35. signal_crp_high is referenced in evaluator tests (backend/tests/unit/test_signal_evaluator.py line 1502) and narrative tests (test_med_rev2_domain_card_copy_and_regeneration.py line 59) but does NOT feed root cause compiler. wave1_cv_vascular_strain card is hidden_v1 per MED-REV-1 — CRP card surface is suppressed from user-facing DTO.
